usage.note: re-run of 63 invalid rows; prior run judge_calls=150

rows.title: Asset dependencies across code locations
rows.reason: A correctly cites AssetSpec/deps for cross-location deps, while B invents a nonexistent ExternalAsset constructor API.

rows.title: Code location instability - gRPC Error code: UNAVAILABLE
rows.reason: Both miss the reference's core point that jobs don't talk to the user code server, but B uses a real env var (DAGSTER_GRPC_MAX_WORKERS) while A fabricates most config options.

rows.title: How to clenup table event_logs?
rows.reason: A gives valid SQL cleanup grounded in real Dagster docs, while B fabricates non-existent retention config and CLI commands contradicting the reference's note that no retention policy exists.

rows.title: Using PythonScriptComponent (or any other dagster feature), can I call a python script with different python dependencies than the dagster user-deployment?
rows.reason: Both correctly identify the venv + subprocess solution; A aligns more closely with Dagster's Pipes-based approach matching the reference, though both have minor API inaccuracies.

rows.title: Is there a Celery-specific RunLauncher (without k8s)?
rows.reason: Both correct that no pure Celery launcher exists and miss the 'required with celery_k8s_job_executor' detail, but B's architecture framing (k8s launcher + Celery step queue) is more accurate than A's.

rows.title: Partition by row id
rows.reason: Reference recommends dynamic partitions populated via a sensor, which B implements exactly while A recommends static partitions.

rows.title: How can I use the cli correctly？
rows.reason: Neither nails DAGSTER_HOME, but B gestures at the shared-instance issue via instance info/path checks while A only reframes execute vs launch.

rows.title: Multiple code locations in Dagster OSS with shared code (resources, utils)
rows.reason: Both recommend the shared-package pattern but miss uv workspaces; A correctly adds executable_path for OSS env isolation while B's run steps undercut true isolation.

rows.title: Structured / json logging by default in Dagster+
rows.reason: A correctly points to dagster.yaml with custom formatters and no custom container, closer to reference; B recommends Python dictConfig and a custom agent container, which the reference explicitly says is unnecessary.

rows.title: Multiprocess slow start for ops
rows.reason: Neither mentions the key forkserver start_method solution, but A's focus on module reimport costs and lazy imports is slightly closer to the root cause.

rows.title: How can I fetch data about all of my historical sensor ticks?
rows.reason: Both wrongly claim a separate sensor_ticks table; the reference confirms job_ticks is correct and the issue is retention settings, which neither mentions, but A hedges while B fabricates confidently.

rows.title: How to use `run_requests` and `dynamic_partitions_requests` with `SensorResult`
rows.reason: B uses the correct AddDynamicPartitionsRequest class (though param name is slightly off), while A uses the wrong DynamicPartitionsRequest class.

rows.title: Confusion about the Dagster hybrid deployment: agents, launchers, executors, workers
rows.reason: Both wrongly say agent isn't the launcher and celery works, but B substantively answers the questions while A mostly punts.

rows.title: Change Dagster log level on runtime
rows.reason: B matches the reference run_config approach exactly, while A uses standard logging which doesn't reliably control Dagster's console logger level.

rows.title: How to define an unpartitioned asset that depends on all upstream partitions
rows.reason: B correctly captures the automatic all-partitions dependency matching the reference, while A pushes an incorrect partition-range approach and admits it's undocumented.

rows.title: Unit Testing ConfigurableResource Classes
rows.reason: Both miss the reference's class-level patching trick, but A's alternatives are sound while B contains a fabricated method (with_init_resource_context).

rows.title: How to programmatically determine logKey for capturedLogsMetadata GraphQL query?
rows.reason: B correctly identifies the logsForRun/LogsCapturedEvent approach matching the reference, while A invents a computeLogs field and incorrect logKey construction.

rows.title: Move materialization history when moving assets from one code location to another
rows.reason: Both contradict the reference that history is preserved by asset key; A is about dbt entirely off-topic, B is at least Dagster-specific but wrongly claims history breaks.

rows.title: How to achieve that dbt assets depends on python assets?
rows.reason: Both wrongly claim @dbt_assets supports a deps parameter rather than the reference's dbt sources approach, but B hedges with caveats.

rows.title: How to add private Python packages to a Dagster cloud deployment?
rows.reason: Answer A matches the reference's wheel-building fast-deploy approach with PAT and pip.conf, while Answer B proposes entirely different methods not aligned with the accepted solution.

rows.title: How do I run a downstream op even if an upstream op fails?
rows.reason: Both capture the try/except upstream catching pattern, but B adds incorrect claims about failure hooks and Nothing dependencies allowing downstream to continue.

rows.title: Examples for k8s configuration
rows.reason: B accurately describes the dagster-k8s/config tag customization approach matching the reference, while A's pod_spec_override syntax is partly inaccurate.

rows.title: dagster-dbt is not respecting`--indirect-selection cautious` test selection
rows.reason: B correctly identifies that asset checks bypass indirect selection and suggests disabling them, while A's main root-cause claim about missing --select is wrong.

rows.title: How to load dbt source description and metadata into Dagster
rows.reason: B correctly explains sources need defining as Dagster assets with metadata, matching the reference, while A wrongly claims auto-parsing should work.

rows.title: How to unit-test `asset_sensor`s
rows.reason: A correctly uses ephemeral instance and addresses the key need to materialize an asset to create events, while B misses the materialization point and is hedging/incomplete.

rows.title: How to get 1-to-1 DeclarativeAutomation.eager() with partitioned asset?
rows.reason: Neither identifies the real fix (removing in_latest_time_window); B is slightly closer by addressing partition evaluation and offering alternatives.

rows.title: Handle assets that are too big to fit in memory
rows.reason: A matches the reference's batch-within-a-single-asset approach with generators, while B's separate-asset design still passes full lists in memory and contains buggy yield/return code.

rows.title: How to silence the ExperimentalWarning?
rows.reason: Both correctly use filterwarnings with ExperimentalWarning but neither mentions the BetaWarning rename in later versions; A is slightly clearer.

rows.title: Automation condition to materialize asset once daily after all upstream updated (but not on cron schedule)
rows.reason: B describes the correct on_cron behavior but uses a fabricated method name; A correctly names on_cron but hedges and mischaracterizes it.

rows.reason: A correctly identifies the Dagster execution context and points to API docs, while B completely misunderstands the question as being about the Claude Agent SDK.

rows.reason: Both miss the intended UI 'wipe materializations' feature; A offers more concrete actionable options.

rows.title: My Dagster code is hanging or taking longer than I expect to execute. How can I troubleshoot?
rows.reason: B correctly centers py-spy with dump/record/speedscope/--idle matching the reference, while A misses py-spy as the primary recommendation and focuses on generic profiling.

rows.title: Programmatic access to full run failure logs, inc stdout/stderr

rows.reason: Both correctly state op-to-op streaming is unsupported but neither mentions Dagster Pipes; B covers more alternatives including external streaming.

rows.reason: A fabricates per-agent max_concurrent_runs and tag routing; B correctly identifies agent queues/routing and admits no per-agent limit exists.

rows.title: Linking to other assets from details view
rows.reason: Answer A is generic and not Dagster-specific, while B correctly addresses Dagster and mentions metadata, the core of the reference answer.

rows.title: "Network Configuration is not valid for the given networkMode of this task definition" error when using the EcsRunLauncher
rows.reason: Both miss the run_task_kwargs override solution; B adds fabricated/invalid config making it worse.

rows.title: How to properly trigger a job once other are done?
rows.reason: B correctly uses a @sensor on run_dbt_job checking job success like the reference, while A invents nonexistent APIs (job_sensor, asset_sensor signature) and vague logic.

rows.title: airbyte+ dbt + dagster: dbt upstream dependency issue
rows.reason: A correctly identifies the schedule must include both assets; B's primary fix (dbt sources) is misguided since lineage already works, though its alternative is correct.

rows.title: Create dynamic assets on runtime
rows.reason: Both correctly recommend dynamic partitions; B is cleaner and more complete despite minor API inaccuracies, while A's sensor code uses incorrect APIs and gets cut off.

rows.title: Confused about code locations and kubernetes
rows.reason: B correctly identifies switching the run launcher so runs execute on the gRPC/code-location server (matching the reference's DefaultRunLauncher), while A wrongly conflates executors with run launchers and invents helm keys.
